title: What Good QA Actually Owns
description: A practical breakdown of what strong QA ownership looks like beyond clicking through tickets and counting defects.
promise: Good QA owns confidence, risk, feedback, and the shape of the release conversation.

title: Regression Pass Rate Is Not Release Confidence
description: Why pass rate is useful but dangerous when treated as the main signal for release readiness.
promise: A pass rate is a signal, not a verdict.

title: My Practical Release Readiness Checklist
description: A field-ready checklist for deciding whether a release is actually understood, not merely finished.
promise: A release checklist should expose risk, not create ceremony.

title: How I Review A Failed Nightly Automation Run
description: A repeatable triage pattern for making failed automation runs useful instead of noisy.
promise: A failed nightly run is only useful when it becomes a decision record.

title: When Not To Automate A Test
description: A practical filter for deciding whether automation will increase confidence or just add maintenance drag.
promise: Automation should buy confidence faster than it spends maintenance.

title: Test Data Is Usually The Real E2E Problem
description: Why E2E testing often fails because of data control, not because the test idea is wrong.
promise: Most E2E pain is data pain wearing a different mask.

title: What Vox Mana Taught Me About Data Contracts
description: How a side project reinforced the value of source layers, generated data boundaries, and explicit contracts.
promise: Side projects become useful when they force you to name the system boundaries.

title: How I Use AI/Codex Without Losing Control Of The Project
description: A practical workflow for using AI assistants while keeping ownership, scope, and decision history clear.
promise: AI is useful when the workflow keeps the human in control of scope and judgment.

title: How I Triage Flaky Automation
description: A repeatable way to classify flaky tests by cause before you touch a single line of test code.
promise: Flaky tests are a signal problem, not a moral failing. Triage them by cause before you rewrite anything.

title: How To Structure A Selenium Test
description: A layered structure that keeps UI tests readable and survivable when the interface changes.
promise: A good Selenium test reads like a sentence and hides its plumbing.

title: How To Build An API Smoke Suite
description: A small, fast, stable suite that decides whether a build is even worth deeper testing.
promise: A smoke suite answers one question fast: is this build worth deeper testing right now?

title: How To Write A Useful Regression Report
description: A report shape that leads with the decision and the blockers instead of a wall of pass/fail counts.
promise: A regression report should drive a decision, not just display numbers.

title: How To Write A Test Plan That Does Not Become Junk
description: A short, decision-oriented test plan that captures risk and scope instead of describing everything.
promise: A test plan earns its keep when it changes what the team does, not when it is filed and forgotten.

title: A Simple QA Dashboard That Leaders Actually Read
description: A dashboard that puts the release decision first and treats charts as supporting evidence.
promise: A leader-readable dashboard answers "can we ship?" before it shows a single chart.

title: A Better Handoff Template For Long-Running Projects
description: A handoff structure that transfers decisions and context, not just files and repositories.
promise: A handoff should let the next person recover context, not just inherit files.

title: Turning Side Projects Into Proof Of Engineering Judgment
description: How to make a side project demonstrate judgment and tradeoffs, not just that you can finish things.
promise: A side project becomes proof when it shows judgment, not just that you can finish things.
